Manx: T’eh er n’yannoo laane taitnys dooys dy akin ayns y “Ghrian” dy vel y Sheshaght Gailkagh er n’irree seose ec-y-jerrey veih nyn merrioose.
English: It has given me much pleasure to notice in the “Sun” that the Manx Language Society have risen at last out of their drowsiness.

Manx: Bleeaney lurg bleeaney va goll harish as cha row veg re-akin vouesyn.
English: Year after year was passing away and yet nothing was seen done.

Manx: Heill me dy row ad ooilley cooidjagh, banglaneyn shyrgit, ceau magh duillagyn beggey laa sy vlein as goll dys cadley son bleiney elley.
English: I thought they were altogether withered branches, casting forth stunted leaves for a single day, then falling asleep for another year.

Manx: Fy-yerree t’eh jeeaghyn, dy vel red-ennagh jeant, as ayns y boayl kiart, son ayns y paitchyn ny-lomarcan ta nyn treishteil.
English: At last, it seems there is something done and in the right place, for in the children is our only hope.

Manx: Ta palchey sleih foast ayns dy-choilley ennyd s’yn Ellan lesh Gailch mie oc dy chur daue.
English: There are plenty of folks still in all parts of the Island who can learn them good Manx.
